summary: Gruber announces the transition of The Talk Show podcast to a new home at Mule Radio Syndicate, providing logistical details for listeners. He shares the new RSS feed, iTunes links, and sponsorship information ($2,500 per spot, two per episode). He thanks debut sponsors, guests, and the Mule Radio team. He also offers a gracious farewell to Dan Benjamin and the 5by5 network, crediting Dan with the vision of targeting the best rather than biggest tech podcast audience.
key_points: The Talk Show moved to Mule Radio Syndicate, requiring listeners to update their RSS subscriptions
Gruber is selling sponsorships directly at $2,500 per spot, two spots per episode, pitching it as reaching 'the most-discerning podcast audience in the world'
He credits Dan Benjamin with the insight that targeting the best tech audience rather than the biggest would work as a podcast network strategy
The post reveals Gruber's approach to podcast business: direct sponsorship sales, honest listener reviews, and personal relationships with sponsors
money_quotes: If you have a product or service you'd like to promote to the most-discerning podcast audience in the world, send me an email.
Two years ago, Dan had the idea to launch and grow a podcast network targeted not necessarily at the biggest tech/nerd audience, but rather the best tech/nerd audience.
He was right, it worked, and I'm proud The Talk Show was a part of that.
It means a lot to me that all four got on board before hearing an episode.
I'll read the reviews ranked 'most helpful' live on the show this week, no matter what they say.
key_insight: Gruber frames the podcast move as a natural evolution while crediting Dan Benjamin's foundational insight that quality audience matters more than size — a philosophy Gruber clearly carries forward into his direct sponsorship model.
